name: scripts-bundle
summary: Enqueue a JavaScript bundle on front-end and/or admin via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that hooks both wp_enqueue_scripts and admin_enqueue_scripts to enqueue a JS bundle. Reads dependencies and version from a companion .asset.php file (the @wordpress/scripts convention).
problem: You have a built JavaScript bundle that needs to be enqueued in WordPress and you want a single, lifecycle-managed class doing it instead of a stray hook.
appliesWhen: You ship a built JS bundle (Webpack, @wordpress/scripts, Vite, etc.)
You want consistent enqueueing across front-end and admin
You're using or willing to follow the @wordpress/scripts asset.php convention
avoidWhen: The script is a tiny inline snippet (use wp_add_inline_script directly)
The script is third-party with its own enqueueing approach
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Enqueues on both front-end and admin by default — narrow this in shouldEnqueue() if you only need one. Constructor expects pluginUrl and pluginPath — wire those via DI.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that hooks wp_enqueue_scripts and admin_enqueue_scripts
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit shouldEnqueue() to scope where the script loads
Wire pluginUrl/pluginPath via DI (typically constants set during plugin bootstrap)
Build the bundle with @wordpress/scripts to generate the companion .asset.php file

name.aiHint: PascalCase. Suffix with Bundle or Scripts for clarity.

srcPath.description: Path to the JS bundle relative to plugin root
srcPath.aiHint: Match the output path your bundler writes.

assetMetaPath.description: Path to the .asset.php metadata file relative to plugin root
